title: calophyllum peekelii
intro: Calophyllum peekelii, also known as the Peekel's Hardwood or the Yellow Sandawood, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Calophyllaceae. It is native to Papua New Guinea and has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of Calophyllum peekelii include its use in treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Calophyllum peekelii can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including a oil extracted from its seeds, which is used in traditional medicine, and as a tea made from its leaves, which is said to have anti-inflammatory properties.
